task_goal: Classify open entities into exactly one of Character / Object / Concept / Location / TimePoint, and assign an appropriate scope.
global_constraints: Output only entities that are suitable as standalone graph nodes; weak background items, purely propositional items, or entries not worth a node may be omitted.
type must be one of Character / Object / Concept / Location / TimePoint.
Decide using the input text, entity name, and description; do not invent content.
grounding_rules: Use the input text first to determine the entity's true semantic role in the current passage, then assign the type.
Use Character for a concrete individual person-like participant.
Use Location for a concrete physical place.
Use TimePoint for a time expression.
Use Object for a physical item.
Use Concept for an organization, institution, group, abstract framework, or social structure.
Use scope = global for entities that are stable across scenes; use local for scene-bound expressions.
If an item is fundamentally a piece of news, a claim, a one-off utterance content, or a vague list item, prefer omitting it instead of forcing a type.
If an item is only a generic everyday object, a pure time modifier, a descriptive location phrase, or a temporary group label, prefer omitting it.
Prefer stable characters, stable locations, key objects, and high-value concepts such as organizations, families, marriages, or professions.

examples.name: Dewey
examples.description: One of the main characters in the story.

examples.name: the next morning
examples.description: A temporal anchor relative to the current scene.
